bead.title: Stop DDx worker health checks from interpreting routing internals
bead.description: Worker health/status paths should not inspect Fizeau route tables or infer provider eligibility. DDx should either display Fizeau's harness/provider status DTOs directly or call a purpose-built health/status method. Audit cli/internal/server/workers.go and related GraphQL worker surfaces for code that calls ResolveRoute or RouteStatus to infer harness health, candidate availability, or provider cooldown. Keep execution-time routing inside Fizeau Execute. Status-only previews may call ResolveRoute, but worker lifecycle decisions should not duplicate Fizeau routing semantics.
bead.acceptance: 1. cli/internal/server/workers.go does not call Fizeau RouteStatus and does not iterate route candidates. 2. Any remaining ResolveRoute calls in worker code are status/preflight only, documented, and covered by tests that they do not pin or mutate Execute routing. 3. Worker GraphQL/API status uses Fizeau HarnessInfo/ProviderInfo availability fields directly for display. 4. rg -n 'RouteStatus\(|\.Routes|\.Candidates' cli/internal/server/workers.go returns no hits. 5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'Worker|Provider' passes.
